description: Regex patterns for static code analysis detection - ALL 21 LANGUAGES

vulnerabilities.message: SQL query may be vulnerable to injection attacks. Use parameterized queries.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use parameterized queries with SqlParameter or Entity Framework's parameterized methods.

vulnerabilities.message: OS command may be vulnerable to injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate and sanitize user input before passing to system commands. Use allowlists when possible.

vulnerabilities.message: Endpoint may be vulnerable to reflected XSS attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Encode user input before rendering. Use Content() with proper encoding or HtmlEncoder.

vulnerabilities.message: I/O operation may be vulnerable to path injection attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate file paths and use Path.GetFullPath() to resolve paths before operations.

vulnerabilities.message: XML parser may be vulnerable to XXE attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Configure XmlReaderSettings with DtdProcessing.Prohibit and XmlResolver = null.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak cipher algorithm detected. Use AES with CBC or GCM mode.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Aes.Create() with CipherMode.CBC or CipherMode.GCM.

vulnerabilities.message: Cryptographic key size is too small.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use at least 2048-bit keys for RSA and 256-bit for ECDSA.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak SSL/TLS protocol version detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use TLS 1.2 or TLS 1.3 only.

vulnerabilities.message: Password hashing is weak or uses insufficient iterations.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use a strong password hashing algorithm like Argon2, bcrypt, or PBKDF2 with at least 100,000 iterations.

vulnerabilities.message: HTTP redirect may be vulnerable to open redirect attacks.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate redirect URLs against an allowlist of trusted domains.

bugs.message: HTTP response body may not be closed.
bugs.suggestion: Always close the response body with defer resp.Body.Close().

bugs.message: Error value is not checked.
bugs.suggestion: Always check error return values. Handle or explicitly ignore with _ = fn().

bugs.message: Potential nil pointer dereference.
bugs.suggestion: Check for nil before dereferencing pointers.

bugs.message: Goroutine launched without lifecycle control.
bugs.suggestion: Use sync.WaitGroup or channels to manage goroutine lifecycle. Prevent goroutine leaks.

bugs.message: File opened without deferred close.
bugs.suggestion: Use defer file.Close() immediately after opening to prevent resource leaks.

bugs.message: Copy of sync.Mutex detected.
bugs.suggestion: Never copy a sync.Mutex. Pass pointers to structs containing mutexes.

bugs.message: Map iteration order is non-deterministic.
bugs.suggestion: Be aware that map iteration order is random. Do not rely on consistent ordering.

bugs.message: time.Tick leaks resources and cannot be stopped.
bugs.suggestion: Use time.NewTicker instead and call Stop() when done to prevent leaks.

bugs.message: Channel operation may cause deadlock.
bugs.suggestion: Ensure channel operations have proper buffering or use select with default case.

bugs.message: Potential slice bounds issue.
bugs.suggestion: Validate slice indices before access. Use range loops when possible.

bugs.message: defer in loop may cause resource leak.
bugs.suggestion: defer inside loops can accumulate. Extract loop body to a function or handle cleanup explicitly.

bugs.message: Channel close without nil check.
bugs.suggestion: Ensure channel is not nil before closing. Only close from sender.

bugs.message: Context cancellation pattern may have issues.
bugs.suggestion: Always call cancel function returned by WithCancel. Use defer for cleanup.

bugs.message: Slice append/copy may have capacity issues.
bugs.suggestion: Check return value of append. Ensure destination slice has sufficient capacity for copy.

bugs.message: Select with default may cause busy waiting.
bugs.suggestion: Consider using a blocking select or adding a small delay to prevent CPU spinning.

bugs.message: recover() may not catch panics as expected.
bugs.suggestion: recover() only works inside deferred functions. Ensure proper placement.

bugs.message: JSON unmarshaling without error check.
bugs.suggestion: Always check errors from json.Unmarshal. Validate struct tags.

bugs.message: Panic may cause application crash.
bugs.suggestion: Use error returns instead of panic for expected error conditions. Reserve panic for unrecoverable errors.

bugs.message: MustCompile panics on invalid regex.
bugs.suggestion: Use regexp.Compile for dynamic patterns and handle errors gracefully.

bugs.message: Join operations may have edge cases.
bugs.suggestion: Handle empty slices properly. Consider strings.Builder for complex concatenations.

bugs.message: Program exit from non-main package.
bugs.suggestion: Avoid os.Exit outside main(). Return errors to allow proper cleanup.

bugs.message: Temporary file creation may have race conditions.
bugs.suggestion: Use secure temporary file creation. Clean up files after use.

bugs.message: sync.Pool usage may retain objects unexpectedly.
bugs.suggestion: Clear sensitive data before putting objects back in Pool. Objects may be garbage collected.

bugs.message: Atomic operations require proper alignment.
bugs.suggestion: Ensure 64-bit values are 64-bit aligned for atomic operations on 32-bit systems.

bugs.message: init() function may have side effects.
bugs.suggestion: Minimize init() usage. Avoid dependencies between init functions. Consider explicit initialization.

bugs.message: Binary data handling may have endianness issues.
bugs.suggestion: Explicitly specify endianness. Document protocol endianness requirements.

codeSmells.suggestion: Add godoc comment starting with the function/type name. Follow Go documentation conventions.
Avoid repeating package name in identifiers. Use 'http.Client' not 'http.HTTPClient'.
Remove unused parameters or rename to _ if interface requires them.
Avoid empty interface{}. Use specific types or generics for type safety.
Refactor deeply nested code. Extract functions or use early returns.
Break large functions into smaller, focused functions with descriptive names.
Replace magic numbers with named constants. Explain the value's meaning.

codeSmells.message: Error type assertion may be fragile.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use errors.Is() and errors.As() for error checking in Go 1.13+.
Be careful with package-level variables. Consider dependency injection.
Wrap errors with context using fmt.Errorf("...: %w", err) for better debugging.
Break long declarations into multiple lines for readability.
Track technical debt in issue tracker. Address or document the TODO.
Group imports: standard library, third-party, then internal packages.
Accept interfaces, return concrete types. Define interfaces where used, not implemented.
Validate request context, check Content-Type, and handle timeouts properly.
Consider using Encoder with SetEscapeHTML(false) for better control.

codeSmells.message: Direct time.Now usage may hinder testing.
codeSmells.suggestion: Consider injecting a clock interface for testability.
Use structured logging (zap, logrus) with proper log levels.
Check errors from Close(), especially for writable files and network connections.
Consider using empty slice check instead of len() == 0 for clarity.
Provide capacity hint to make() if approximate size is known.
Use index, value := range for both. Avoid modifying slice during iteration.
Respect context cancellation. Don't ignore ctx.Done() signals.
Consider named return values for documentation in longer functions.
Group related constants. Consider using iota for sequential values.

vulnerabilities.message: SOQL injection via string concatenation. User input may be injected into the query.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use bind variables in SOQL queries: [SELECT Id FROM Account WHERE Name = :nameVar]. Never concatenate user input.

vulnerabilities.message: SOSL injection via string concatenation. User input may be injected into the search.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use bind variables in SOSL queries. Avoid concatenating user input into FIND clauses.

vulnerabilities.message: Dynamic SOQL constructed from user input may be vulnerable to injection.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use static SOQL with bind variables. If dynamic SOQL is necessary, strictly validate and escape all inputs.

vulnerabilities.message: Hard-coded credentials detected in source code.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Named Credentials, Custom Metadata, or Protected Custom Settings for secrets. Never hard-code credentials.

vulnerabilities.message: Visualforce page may be vulnerable to XSS. Unescaped user input detected.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Always use escape='true' in apex:outputText. Use HTMLENCODE(), JSENCODE(), or URLENCODE() for dynamic content.

vulnerabilities.message: CRUD/FLS permissions are not being enforced. DML operations may bypass user permissions.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Check Schema.sObjectType.ObjectName.isAccessible()/.isCreateable()/.isUpdateable()/.isDeletable() before DML operations.

vulnerabilities.message: Class does not declare sharing model. May default to 'without sharing' and bypass record-level security.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Explicitly declare 'with sharing' to enforce record-level security, or 'without sharing' with documented justification.

vulnerabilities.message: DML operations should enforce object-level security by checking FLS.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use stripInaccessible() or check Field-Level Security before DML operations on fields.

vulnerabilities.message: Weak cryptographic algorithm detected. Use strong algorithms like AES-256.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use Crypto.encryptWithManagedIV() with AES256. Avoid DES, 3DES, RC4, MD5, and SHA-1.

vulnerabilities.message: Potential open redirect vulnerability. User-controlled URL may redirect to malicious site.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate redirect URLs against an allowlist of trusted domains before redirecting.

securityHotspots.message: Using 'without sharing' or 'inherited sharing' may bypass record-level security. Review security implications.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Prefer 'with sharing' for classes. If 'without sharing' is necessary, document the security justification.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Implement proper CRUD/FLS checks in @RemoteAction methods. Validate user permissions explicitly.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Add 'with sharing' to the class and implement FLS/CRUD checks. Use Security.stripInaccessible() for field-level security.

bugs.message: SOQL query inside a loop. This will hit Salesforce governor limits quickly.
bugs.suggestion: Query all records before the loop using a Map or List, then access them inside the loop.

bugs.message: DML operation inside a loop. This will hit Salesforce governor limits (150 DML statements per transaction).
bugs.suggestion: Collect records in a List and perform DML once after the loop using bulk DML operations.

bugs.message: Potential governor limit violation. Code may exceed SOQL queries, DML rows, or CPU time limits.
bugs.suggestion: Review code for nested loops, unnecessary queries, and bulkification. Use Limits class to monitor usage.

bugs.message: HTTP callout inside a loop. This will hit Salesforce governor limits (100 callouts per transaction).
bugs.suggestion: Move callouts outside loops. Batch requests or use @future/@Queueable for asynchronous processing.

bugs.message: Trigger logic should be delegated to handler classes for bulkification and maintainability.
bugs.suggestion: Move trigger logic to a separate handler class. Use TriggerHandler pattern for better organization.

bugs.message: Future methods should not be called from batch classes. This can cause unexpected behavior.
bugs.suggestion: Use Queueable Apex instead of @future in batch contexts, or restructure to avoid nested async operations.

bugs.message: Mixed DML operations on setup and non-setup objects. This will cause a MIXED_DML_OPERATION error.
bugs.suggestion: Separate setup objects (User, Profile, etc.) and non-setup objects DML into different transactions using @future or Queueable.

bugs.message: Potential null pointer or index out of bounds. Query result may be empty.

codeSmells.message: Test assertions may be trivial or not meaningless. Ensure tests validate actual business logic.

codeSmells.suggestion: Use seeAllData=false (default) and create test data with @TestSetup or Test Data Factory.

codeSmells.message: Apex class may have too many responsibilities. Consider breaking into smaller classes.
codeSmells.suggestion: Follow Single Responsibility Principle. Split large classes into focused, single-purpose classes.

codeSmells.suggestion: Add indexed fields (Id, Name, External Ids) to WHERE clause. Use selective filters to avoid hitting limits.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Add USER instruction to run as non-root
Remove sudo, adjust permissions or use proper USER

securityHotspots.message: Using latest tag for base image
securityHotspots.suggestion: Pin to specific version tag for reproducible builds
Always specify a version tag for base images
Add to .dockerignore and use runtime mounting
Use IAM roles or mount credentials at runtime
Use Docker secrets or runtime environment injection

securityHotspots.message: .npmrc with potential auth token in image
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use BuildKit secrets for npm authentication

bugs.message: Multiple CMD instructions - only last takes effect
bugs.suggestion: Consolidate into single CMD or use ENTRYPOINT

bugs.message: Multiple ENTRYPOINT instructions - only last takes effect
bugs.suggestion: Consolidate into single ENTRYPOINT

bugs.message: Deprecated MAINTAINER instruction
bugs.suggestion: Use LABEL maintainer="..." instead

bugs.message: WORKDIR using relative path
bugs.suggestion: Use absolute paths for WORKDIR

bugs.message: ARG before FROM not available after
bugs.suggestion: Repeat ARG after FROM if needed in that stage

bugs.message: Port number out of valid range (1-65535)
bugs.suggestion: Use valid port numbers between 1-65535

bugs.message: Shell form with variables may not work as expected
bugs.suggestion: Use JSON array form for CMD/ENTRYPOINT

vulnerabilities.message: Script injection via untrusted GitHub event data.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Store event data in an environment variable first, then reference the variable.

vulnerabilities.message: Secret may be exposed in CI/CD configuration or logs.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use CI/CD secret management. Never echo secrets to logs.

vulnerabilities.message: Action or image not pinned to a specific SHA or immutable version.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Pin actions to full commit SHA and images to digest for supply chain security.

vulnerabilities.message: Overly permissive token permissions.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Follow least-privilege principle. Grant only necessary permissions.

vulnerabilities.message: Potentially dangerous trigger. pull_request_target runs with write access.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Be cautious with pull_request_target. Never checkout untrusted PR code with this trigger.

vulnerabilities.message: Downloading and executing remote scripts in pipeline.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Download, verify integrity with checksums, then execute.

vulnerabilities.message: Secret may be leaked to environment or logs.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Never write secrets to GITHUB_ENV or log output. Use masked outputs.

vulnerabilities.message: Hardcoded password detected in CI/CD configuration
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use secrets management or environment variables with masking

vulnerabilities.message: Hardcoded API key detected in pipeline
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Store API keys in secret management

vulnerabilities.message: SSH private key detected in pipeline file
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use SSH keys stored in secrets management

vulnerabilities.message: Sudo without password detected
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Configure sudo with NOPASSWD only for specific commands

vulnerabilities.message: Dangerous curl | bash pattern detected
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Download script, verify checksum, then execute

vulnerabilities.message: Untrusted third-party GitHub Action detected
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Pin to specific commit SHA and verify action source

vulnerabilities.message: Unpinned action reference
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Pin actions to specific commit SHA

vulnerabilities.message: Self-hosted runner detected
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use ephemeral runners with proper isolation

vulnerabilities.message: Docker socket mounted in container
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid mounting Docker socket; use Docker-in-Docker

vulnerabilities.message: Privileged container execution
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Remove privileged mode; use capabilities

vulnerabilities.message: eval with variable input detected
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid eval; use safer alternatives

codeSmells.message: Step configured to continue on error. Failures may be silently ignored.
codeSmells.suggestion: Remove continue-on-error unless explicitly needed and documented.
Use reasonable timeouts to catch hanging processes early.
Track this task in your issue tracker.

codeSmells.message: Step runs regardless of previous step outcome.
codeSmells.suggestion: Consider using more specific conditions to avoid unnecessary runs on failures.
Include a cache key with a hash of lock files for deterministic caching.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Use principle of least privilege
Use IAM roles or AWS Secrets Manager

bugs.message: Potential YAML syntax error
bugs.suggestion: Validate YAML syntax

bugs.message: Environment variable may not be defined
bugs.suggestion: Define all required environment variables

vulnerabilities.message: Command injection via eval. Arbitrary code execution is possible.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Avoid eval entirely. Use arrays and proper quoting instead.

vulnerabilities.message: Unquoted variable. Word splitting and globbing may occur.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Always double-quote variables: "$var" or "${var}".

vulnerabilities.message: Dangerous rm command that may delete system files.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate paths before deletion. Never use rm -rf with variable paths or root directory.

vulnerabilities.message: Insecure curl/wget usage. TLS verification disabled or piping to shell.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Remove -k/--insecure flags. Download to file, verify checksum, then execute.

vulnerabilities.message: SSH host key verification is disabled. Man-in-the-middle attacks possible.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Enable StrictHostKeyChecking. Use proper known_hosts file management.

vulnerabilities.message: Dangerous file deletion with variable path. May delete unintended files.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Validate paths before deletion. Use rm with --preserve-root and --one-file-system.

vulnerabilities.message: Hard-coded credentials detected. Security risk if code is exposed.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use environment variables or a secrets manager. Never hard-code credentials.

vulnerabilities.message: HTTP or other clear-text protocol detected. Data may be intercepted.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use HTTPS instead of HTTP for all network communications.

vulnerabilities.message: Overly permissive file permissions (world-writable).
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use restrictive permissions (644 for files, 755 for directories). Avoid 777.

vulnerabilities.message: Sudo usage detected. Privilege escalation risk.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Minimize sudo usage. Validate commands before execution with elevated privileges.

vulnerabilities.message: Insecure OpenSSL encryption usage.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use proper key derivation (PBKDF2) and always use salt. Consider using gpg instead.

vulnerabilities.message: Base64/encoding used - this is not encryption.
vulnerabilities.suggestion: Use proper encryption (openssl, gpg) for sensitive data, not just encoding.

securityHotspots.suggestion: Add shebang (#!/bin/bash or #!/bin/sh) as the first line.

securityHotspots.message: Backticks used instead of $(). Backticks are deprecated.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use $() instead of backticks for command substitution. It's nestable and more readable.

securityHotspots.message: Deprecated -a/-o test operators. Not POSIX compliant.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use && and || between test expressions instead of -a and -o.

securityHotspots.message: Using 'which' to check command existence is not portable.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use 'command -v' instead of 'which' for POSIX compliance.

securityHotspots.message: echo with flags is not portable across systems.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use printf instead of echo for portable, formatted output.

securityHotspots.message: Sourcing files with relative paths may load malicious code.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use absolute paths when sourcing files or validate the path.

securityHotspots.message: PATH modification detected. Current directory in PATH is dangerous.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Never include current directory (.) in PATH. Validate PATH changes.

securityHotspots.message: Custom prompt modification may hide malicious activity.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Review prompt modifications for potential command obfuscation.

securityHotspots.message: Alias definition may mask commands or create confusion.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Use functions instead of aliases for complex operations. Document all aliases.

securityHotspots.message: Signal trap detected. May hide errors or prevent normal termination.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Ensure trap handlers properly clean up and don't mask critical errors.

securityHotspots.message: IFS modification detected. May affect word splitting behavior.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Save and restore IFS properly. Limit scope of IFS changes.

securityHotspots.message: Exec command may replace shell process or redirect critical file descriptors.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Validate exec arguments carefully. Document file descriptor redirects.

securityHotspots.message: Named pipe (FIFO) creation detected. May be used for inter-process communication.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Ensure FIFO permissions are restrictive. Clean up FIFOs after use.

securityHotspots.message: Netcat listener detected. Potential backdoor or unauthorized access.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Ensure netcat usage is authorized. Use proper authentication mechanisms.

securityHotspots.message: Docker command with variable input. Container escape risk.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Validate all docker arguments. Avoid privileged mode. Use specific image tags.

securityHotspots.message: kubectl command with variable input. Cluster security risk.
securityHotspots.suggestion: Validate kubectl arguments. Use RBAC. Avoid running privileged containers.

bugs.message: Variable is self-assigned. This has no effect.
bugs.suggestion: Remove the self-assignment or fix the logic.

bugs.message: Unquoted variable in test/comparison. May cause syntax errors with empty values.
bugs.suggestion: Quote variables in test expressions: [ "$var" = "value" ].

bugs.message: Piping to while loop creates a subshell. Variables set inside will be lost.
bugs.suggestion: Use process substitution: while read line; do ... done < <(command).

bugs.message: Using == in [ ] (POSIX test). Not portable.
bugs.suggestion: Use = for POSIX compatibility or use [[ ]] for bash-specific features.

bugs.message: local declaration without initialization may mask errors.
bugs.suggestion: Initialize local variables: local var="value" or local var; var=...

bugs.message: read without -r flag may mangle backslashes.
bugs.suggestion: Use read -r for raw input to preserve backslashes.

bugs.message: cd with variable or relative path may fail if directory doesn't exist.
bugs.suggestion: Check if directory exists before cd: cd "$dir" || exit 1

bugs.message: Word splitting in for loop may cause unexpected iteration.
bugs.suggestion: Quote the variable or use arrays: for item in "$@"; do ...

bugs.message: Unquoted "$*" or "$@" may cause word splitting issues.
bugs.suggestion: Use "$@" for iterating over arguments or "$*" for joining with IFS.

bugs.message: Command substitution inside grep/sed/awk may fail with special characters.
bugs.suggestion: Pass data via stdin or use -v flag to define variables.

codeSmells.suggestion: Track this task in your issue tracker and remove the TODO comment when done.

codeSmells.message: Script may be missing set -euo pipefail for strict error handling.
codeSmells.suggestion: Add 'set -euo pipefail' after the shebang for safer script execution.

codeSmells.message: Script uses /bin/sh which may be less feature-rich than bash.
codeSmells.suggestion: Consider using #!/bin/bash if you need bash-specific features, or ensure POSIX compliance.

codeSmells.message: Useless use of cat (UUOC). Programs can read files directly.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use 'grep pattern file' instead of 'cat file | grep pattern'.

codeSmells.message: Multiple piped grep/awk commands can be combined.
codeSmells.suggestion: Combine patterns: grep -E 'pattern1|pattern2' or use a single awk.

codeSmells.message: expr is deprecated and slow. Use $(( )) arithmetic expansion.
codeSmells.suggestion: Replace expr with $(( )) or let for arithmetic operations.

codeSmells.message: seq is external. Use bash built-in {1..n} or C-style for loop.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use bash brace expansion or C-style for loop instead of seq.

codeSmells.message: function keyword is not POSIX compliant.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use POSIX syntax: myfunc() { ... } instead of function myfunc { ... }

codeSmells.message: Sleep may indicate race conditions or poor synchronization.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use proper synchronization mechanisms instead of arbitrary sleep delays.

codeSmells.message: Manual exit code check may indicate missing error handling.
codeSmells.suggestion: Use 'set -e' or check exit codes with if statements for proper error handling.
